pending.body:
finding_summary: The new beads health guard only runs inside `handle_propose_new_bead`; the normal bead-closing and bead-claiming flows still mutate `.beads` without any preflight validation.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2998-3029
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:204-216
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:377-430
- src/cli/project.rs:964-981
remediation_hint: Move the health check into a shared helper and invoke it before every milestone mutation entrypoint that updates or closes bead state, not just before proposed-bead creation, so conflicted or missing `.beads/issues.jsonl` stops the operation before `br update`/`br close`/`br sync` runs.

finding_summary: `check_beads_health()` misidentifies ordinary `issues.jsonl` content as a merge conflict because it uses raw substring checks for `<<<<<<<`, `=======`, and `>>>>>>>`, and it also collapses every non-`NotFound` read failure into `ConflictMarkers`.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/adapters/br_health.rs:25-31
- src/adapters/br_health.rs:45-46
remediation_hint: Detect real conflict-marker lines instead of any substring match, and preserve other read failures as distinct errors so operators are not told to resolve a nonexistent merge conflict.

Bug in br_health.rs:31 — the catch-all `Err(_) => return BeadsHealthStatus::ConflictMarkers` misclassifies non-NotFound I/O errors (permission denied, disk errors) as conflict markers. The downstream error message in service.rs:3004 then tells the operator to "resolve the conflict" when the real problem is unreadable file state. Fix: either add a new `BeadsHealthStatus::IoError` variant, or fall through to `BrUnavailable` which at least doesn't produce an actively wrong diagnosis. The fail-safe blocking behavior is correct, only the reported reason is wrong.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 2 -> Plan and Implement
